intent.feature_concept: Reusable horizontal tab-strip primitive for switching panels within a pane — tabs array + active id, optional count/label badge per tab, optional right-aligned action cluster, emits change events
intent.topic: Reusable horizontal tab-strip primitive for switching panels within a pane — tabs array + active id, optional count/label badge per tab, optional right-aligned action cluster, emits change events
intent.scope_summary: Reusable, controlled, HORIZONTAL-only tab-strip primitive (no vertical variant): renders a row of tab buttons (label + optional badge + active state) from a tabs array, marks the activeId, emits onChange(id) on click or keyboard select, supports an optional right-aligned actions slot, implements roving-tabindex keyboard nav (arrows/Home/End with wrap) + WAI-ARIA tablist/tab semantics, skips disabled tabs. Selection only — does not render or own the panels it switches.
intent.verbatim_prompt: A reusable horizontal tab-strip for switching panels within a pane: a row of tab buttons each with a label, an optional count/label badge, and an active state, plus an optional right-aligned action-button cluster. Driven by a tabs array and an active id; emits change events. Used by the request authoring pane (Params/Auth/Headers/Body/Tests/Code) and the response pane (Body/Headers/Cookies/Test Results). Tabs benefit from roving-tabindex keyboard navigation and ARIA tab semantics, so consider a headless tabs primitive (Radix Tabs, React Aria) versus a hand-rolled strip — it is light enough that hand-rolled is reasonable; let build-vs-buy decide. For visual and interaction consistency, follow the same approach the existing UI primitives in specs/001-ui-primitives used (the renderer already has Dropdown/Modal/Toast/Icon and, if a headless library was adopted there, reuse that same library's Tabs rather than introducing a second one). Selection only; the panels it switches are separate tasks. Visual reference: design/reference.html — look and behavior only, never reproduce its markup (data-om-* attributes, __OmT wrappers, inline styles, tweaks-panel must not enter the build). Style with semantic class names from role and design/tokens.json.

constraints.content: Controlled-only: caller owns active id and updates it via onChange; strip holds no internal active state (matches 001 Dropdown/Modal controlled pattern). Plus inherited project hard limits: strictly typed (no any); no inline styles; semantic class names mapped to design/tokens.json; prefers-reduced-motion gating; never reproduce design/reference.html markup (no data-om-*/__OmT/inline styles/tweaks-panel); renderer-only (no Node/Electron imports); WAI-ARIA tabs pattern with roving tabindex.

Out of scope: OUT of scope: the panels/panel content it switches (separate tasks); overflow handling (scroll/truncate for too-many-tabs); vertical orientation; uncontrolled/self-managed state; selected-tab routing or persistence; reproducing design/reference.html markup; introducing a second tabs library if Radix Tabs fits; animated selected-tab indicator or sliding-underline transitions (static selected-tab styling only, still respecting prefers-reduced-motion if any transition exists).
§2.3 / Never import Node or electron in renderer

semantic-class composer Tabs reuses for BEM class assembly
reused if a per-tab leading icon is ever added; consistent with Dropdown item icons
Tabs registered here for manual visual fidelity check vs design/reference.html
radix-ui ^1.1.3 already declared; Tabs namespace available with no new dependency
internal — canonical controlled-primitive-wrapping-Radix pattern to extend for Tabs (cx + Icon + BEM + import './X.css')
internal — same controlled Radix-namespace wrapper pattern; Provider/Viewport split, store-driven
internal — controlled Radix wrapper primitive; focus trap, prefers-reduced-motion gating precedent
internal — shared semantic-class composer used by every primitive; Tabs reuses it

risks.risk: Spike: render Radix Tabs.Root+List+Trigger as a selection-only strip WITHOUT Tabs.Content; run axe/a11y to check for dangling aria-controls and decide share-one-Root vs hand-rolled tablist
risks.mitigation: address before implementation; see derisk plan

risks.risk: Contract test against both panes tab sets: click + arrows/Home/End wrap (List loop), disabled-skip, onChange(id) fires once, aria-selected/tablist roles correct, invalid/absent activeId renders no selection
risks.mitigation: address before implementation; see derisk plan

risks.risk: Register in PrimitivesDemo.tsx and visually compare to design/reference.html at request-pane (6 tabs) and response-pane (4 tabs) widths
risks.mitigation: address before implementation; see derisk plan

risks.risk: Radix Tabs couples Trigger->Content via aria-controls; a standalone selection-only strip risks dangling aria-controls unless panels join the same Tabs.Root or the tablist is hand-rolled

open_questions.question: user-supplied mechanism guess (confirm via Phase 2 fit-check): reuse whatever headless tabs library specs/001-ui-primitives adopted (Dropdown/Modal/Toast/Icon) rather than introducing a second; assumes 001 adopted a headless lib at all

design_options.name: Array-driven controlled strip
design_options.shape: Flat props: <Tabs tabs={[{id,label,badge?,disabled?}]} activeId onChange actions?/>. One component renders the whole strip from the array; caller owns activeId and handles onChange(id). Mirrors Dropdown's items-array API.
pros: Matches the API the user described (tabs array + active id)
Smallest consumer surface; one line to mount
Direct precedent in Dropdown items-array API
cons: Less composable for one-off custom tab content
Badge limited to string|number unless widened

design_options.name: Compound components
design_options.shape: Children API: <Tabs.Root activeId onChange><Tabs.List><Tabs.Tab id badge/>...</Tabs.List><Tabs.Actions/></Tabs.Root>. Composition mirrors Radix and Dropdown's children API; each tab is JSX.
pros: Maximum composition flexibility (custom tab nodes, arbitrary actions)
cons: More verbose at every call site
More exported parts to document and test
Diverges from the simple array the user asked for

design_options.name: Array + slot nodes
design_options.shape: Array-driven like option A, but badge accepts a ReactNode and actions is a render slot; tabs={[{id,label,badge?:ReactNode,disabled?}]} actions={<.../>}. Hybrid of A's ergonomics and B's flexibility.
pros: Keeps the simple array call site
Allows rich badges / custom action clusters without compound parts
cons: ReactNode badge complicates ARIA labelling and tests
Two ways to express simple cases invites inconsistency

build_vs_buy.build_path: Hand-roll the strip: role=tablist + role=tab buttons, manual roving tabindex (arrows/Home/End with wrap), aria-selected, disabled-skip, onChange(id). Full panel-decoupling (no aria-controls coupling), but reimplements well-trodden a11y and carries the larger test surface.
build_vs_buy.buy_path: Wrap radix-ui's Tabs namespace (Tabs.Root + Tabs.List + Tabs.Trigger) behind the flat array API. Inherits controlled value/onValueChange, roving tabindex, List loop (keyboard wrap), Trigger disabled-skip, activationMode, and WAI-ARIA for free; no new dependency. Must manage Trigger->Content aria-controls for the selection-only strip.
build_vs_buy.reasoning: Buy the accessibility engine (Radix Tabs, already a dependency), build the thin project veneer (the flat array wrapper + semantic classes) — exactly how Dropdown/Modal/Toast already treat Radix. This minimizes new a11y code and stays consistent with the codebase. The only design-time work is reconciling aria-controls with the panels-separate scope; pure hand-rolling is the fallback if that reconciliation proves awkward.

cited_canonical_patterns.relevance: internal — canonical controlled-primitive-wrapping-Radix pattern to extend for Tabs (cx + Icon + BEM + import './X.css')
internal — same controlled Radix-namespace wrapper pattern; Provider/Viewport split, store-driven
internal — controlled Radix wrapper primitive; focus trap, prefers-reduced-motion gating precedent
internal — shared semantic-class composer used by every primitive; Tabs reuses it
internal — visual-verification route where each primitive is registered; success_criteria requires Tabs added here

Controlled value/onValueChange, roving tabindex, Tabs.List loop=keyboard wrap, Trigger disabled-skip, activationMode auto/manual, horizontal default, full WAI-ARIA — covers nearly all gap behavior; already a dep (no new install). Caveat: Trigger sets aria-controls expecting a Tabs.Content.
Authoritative spec for the hand-rolled path: role=tablist/tab, aria-selected, roving tabindex, arrow/Home/End, automatic-vs-manual activation guidance
Alternative headless tabs hook; would be a SECOND a11y lib alongside Radix — violates the 'no second library' guidance, recorded for completeness

plan_seeds.recommended_option_rationale: It matches the API the user described (tabs array + active id + onChange) and extends the established controlled-primitive wrapper pattern at internal:src/renderer/src/components/molecules/Dropdown.tsx — those primitives implement the Radix-wrapper pattern but NOT tab-strip selection, which is the capability this option adds. Tradeoff accepted: badge stays string|number and one-off custom tab content is unsupported; if a richer badge is ever needed, widen to option C without breaking the array call site.

discovery_block.fit_rationale: Strong fit. The controlled-primitive-wrapping-Radix pattern is established three times in src/renderer/src/components/molecules/ and radix-ui (which ships a Tabs namespace) is already a dependency, so the user's 'reuse it, no second lib' belief is confirmed and the build is small. The single real wrinkle is design-time, not effort: Radix Tabs couples Trigger->Content via aria-controls, which tensions the selection-only/panels-separate scope — resolved by either sharing one Tabs.Root with consumer panels or hand-rolling the tablist. Either path stays within the existing pattern and needs no new dependency.

fit_assessments.user_expected: new tab-strip lives beside Dropdown/Modal/Toast and follows the same wrapper pattern
fit_assessments.reality: confirmed — molecules/ holds the three controlled Radix-wrapper primitives; Tabs.tsx + Tabs.css drop in cleanly with cx()+Icon+import './Tabs.css'

fit_assessments.user_expected: reuse whatever headless lib 001 adopted; do not add a second one
fit_assessments.reality: confirmed — radix-ui ^1.1.3 is adopted and exposes a Tabs namespace; no new dependency needed. BUT Radix Tabs.Trigger emits aria-controls expecting a sibling Tabs.Content; selection-only (panels separate) means either consumers share one Tabs.Root with their panels, or the strip hand-rolls role=tablist to stay panel-decoupled
blockers: Radix Tabs couples Trigger->Content via aria-controls; a standalone selection-only strip risks dangling aria-controls unless panels join the same Tabs.Root or the tablist is hand-rolled

fit_assessments.user_expected: register Tabs in the demo for manual visual verification
fit_assessments.reality: confirmed — PrimitivesDemo.tsx already registers each primitive; success_criteria's manual visual check plugs in here

fit_assessments.user_expected: reuse cx() for semantic BEM classes
fit_assessments.reality: confirmed — cx() at src/renderer/src/lib/cx.ts is used by every primitive

functional_scope.value: Reusable, controlled, HORIZONTAL-only tab-strip primitive (no vertical variant): renders a row of tab buttons (label + optional badge + active state) from a tabs array, marks the activeId, emits onChange(id) on click or keyboard select, supports an optional right-aligned actions slot, implements roving-tabindex keyboard nav (arrows/Home/End with wrap) + WAI-ARIA tablist/tab semantics, skips disabled tabs. Selection only — does not render or own the panels it switches.

users.value: General-purpose reusable primitive for renderer developers: first consumers are the request authoring pane (Params/Auth/Headers/Body/Tests/Code) and the response pane (Body/Headers/Cookies/Test Results), but designed without coupling to those two call sites so any future pane/panel switching can use it. End user is the app user clicking/keyboarding tabs. No external API consumers.

inputs_outputs.value: Inputs: tabs array of { id: string, label: string, badge?: string|number, disabled?: boolean }; activeId: string (controlled); optional actions slot (ReactNode, right-aligned cluster). Outputs: onChange(id: string) fired on click or keyboard select; disabled tabs are non-selectable and skipped by keyboard nav. No panel content crosses the boundary (panels are separate).

integration_points.value: New Tabs.tsx + Tabs.css in src/renderer/src/components/molecules/ (same home as Dropdown/Modal/Toast), using the cx() helper (src/renderer/src/lib/cx.ts) + Icon atom, registered in PrimitivesDemo.tsx for manual visual verification. Build mechanism (radix-ui Tabs namespace vs hand-rolled roving-tabindex) is LEFT OPEN for Phase 2/3 build-vs-buy on the evidence. radix-ui ^1.1.3 is already a dependency (adopted by 001), so the 'reuse it, no second lib' guess is materially supported — confirmed in Phase 2 fit-check.

constraints.value: Controlled-only: caller owns active id and updates it via onChange; strip holds no internal active state (matches 001 Dropdown/Modal controlled pattern). Plus inherited project hard limits: strictly typed (no any); no inline styles; semantic class names mapped to design/tokens.json; prefers-reduced-motion gating; never reproduce design/reference.html markup (no data-om-*/__OmT/inline styles/tweaks-panel); renderer-only (no Node/Electron imports); WAI-ARIA tabs pattern with roving tabindex.

non_goals.value: OUT of scope: the panels/panel content it switches (separate tasks); overflow handling (scroll/truncate for too-many-tabs); vertical orientation; uncontrolled/self-managed state; selected-tab routing or persistence; reproducing design/reference.html markup; introducing a second tabs library if Radix Tabs fits; animated selected-tab indicator or sliding-underline transitions (static selected-tab styling only, still respecting prefers-reduced-motion if any transition exists).

success_criteria.value: Behavior + keyboard nav + ARIA semantics gated by interaction tests (renders both panes' tab sets from a tabs array; click + arrows/Home/End switch active; WAI-ARIA tablist/tab/aria-selected correct; change event fires). Visual fidelity to design/reference.html verified manually via the PrimitivesDemo route.

edge_cases.value: Must handle: (a) invalid/absent active id — id not in tabs array, empty, or null → render with no tab selected (decision deferred to design); (b) empty tabs array → render empty strip, and single tab → still a valid tablist; (c) keyboard arrow nav wrap at first/last tab, and badge extremes (0, large counts, or a text label not a number). Overflow/too-many-tabs is OUT of scope (request pane = 6 tabs, response = 4; no scroll/truncate handling).

references: Radix Tabs (radix-ui unified package, already a dep)

gaps.description: user-supplied mechanism guess (confirm via Phase 2 fit-check): reuse whatever headless tabs library specs/001-ui-primitives adopted (Dropdown/Modal/Toast/Icon) rather than introducing a second; assumes 001 adopted a headless lib at all
